title: Repeal the TikTok Ban Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
plain_summary: This bill removes a law that was made to control apps owned by countries that are enemies of the United States. The old law put restrictions on certain apps and websites to keep them from operating in America. This new bill takes away all of those restrictions and lets those apps work freely again. It completely gets rid of the rules that were blocking foreign-owned apps from being used in the United States.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Repeal the TikTok Ban Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Repeal of Protecting Americans from Foreign Adversary Controlled Applications Act
sections.text: This section cancels an existing law called the Protecting Americans from Foreign Adversary Controlled Applications Act. This existing law was created to control apps that the government believes are controlled by foreign enemies. The section has two main parts. First, the entire existing law is completely removed and no longer has any power. Second, any app or website that was previously labeled as being controlled by a foreign enemy under the old law will no longer have that label. This means those apps and websites can operate normally again, as if they were never restricted.
